findings.finding: SKILL.md serialization order (Task 5 inserts R15 cross-reference before Task 6 documents the R4 approval gate that gates entry into decompose) leaves intermediate-commit prose with a forward reference to an undocumented gate.
findings.evidence_quote: SKILL.md edits ordering (Tasks 5, 6, 7): three tasks touch skills/discovery/SKILL.md. Plan serializes 5 -> 6 -> 7 to avoid merge conflicts.

findings.finding: Task 7 smoke-test parsers (refine clarify-critic loader, lifecycle discovery-bootstrap loader, backlog index generator) are deferred to grep-time discovery — pass condition unverifiable in advance.
findings.evidence_quote: Locate these via grep -rn discovery_source skills/refine skills/lifecycle cortex_command/ during implementation.

findings.finding: R6 LEX-2 one-reopen-per-cycle stopping rule at risk of being consumed by discovered-late Task-9 (ii-a) failure, leaving zero reopens for post-ship false-positives.
findings.evidence_quote: If the R12 re-walk shows over-merging, the LEX-2 reopen path is open.

findings.finding: Task 5 events-registry verification may fail if registry checker validates producer-file existence and cortex_command/discovery.py is not yet committed.
findings.evidence_quote: Event emission path resolution depends on Task 6 helper module — but the prose just names the event by literal name.

findings.finding: Plan conflates atomic-commit with atomic-task; sub-tasks sharing a terminal commit is a viable decoupling option not surfaced in Veto Surface.
findings.evidence_quote: A task could be decomposed into multiple sub-tasks that build up the working tree, with a terminal sub-task running the commit.

findings.finding: Task 8 (#195 rewrite) works in isolation but cannot generalize — only 3 of 197 backlog tickets currently match the uniform Role/Integration/Edges template, so single-file scanner exit-0 is not corpus-level evidence.
